other_observers: Brandon Percival also saw this bird a couple of hours later.; David Silverman was the original observer of this bird.
description: This bird was a medium sized Tern about same size as nearby Common & Forster's Terns, but it had a longer tail, & smaller head and bill. Thus its "jizz" was different than the other terns, because of its short front end and long extended rear end. This "jizz" was very noticeable in flight compared to the other Terns.  CROWN & FACE: Crown was solid black from nape to base of bill. Black extended over dark eye. Face and throat were white which contrasted with crown & smokey light gray underparts.  WINGS: They were curved, pointed at tips, and exhibited a light grayish mantle. On perched bird the tail extended beyond wing tips. In flight, a thin black line was noticed bordering the primaries, especially on the wing's underside and trailing edge of primaries.  BILL: Bill was short, noticeably shorter than head width from nape to lores, very slender, and scarlet red.   LEGS & FEET: Legs were very short and reddish as were feet.Behaviors: This bird flew around and island, occasionally dived into water to feed, and then perched on rocks near the island.Call: nonePlumage: alternate adult
